Privacy first

Privacy Policy
for STURNET

STURNET supports sturgeon bycatch reporting and species identification. Reports are saved locally first and are uploaded to the STURNET research database only when you choose to synchronise them.

Effective July 10, 2026User-controlled synchronisationNo advertising or data salesVisit sturnet.eu ↗

Contents

  1. Introduction
  2. Information We Collect

At a glance: STURNET stores new reports on your device first. When you choose Sync Now, report data is sent to the STURNET research server for scientific research and sturgeon-conservation work. Photographs remain on your device and are not uploaded.
1

Introduction

STURNET (“we”, “our”, or “the app”) is a mobile application developed for sturgeon bycatch reporting and species identification. This Privacy Policy explains what information is handled by the app, how reports are stored and synchronised, and the choices available to you.

2

Information We Collect

When you choose to synchronise a bycatch report, the following field data is transmitted to and stored on the STURNET server at sturnet.eu for scientific research purposes:

  • GPS location (optional) — if you select “Capture Location,” the latitude and longitude are added to the report and uploaded when the report is synchronised. A report can be created without GPS coordinates.
  • Catch and sighting data — species name, report type, fish count, fish condition, visible-disease information, disease notes, length, weight, and any free-text notes you enter.
  • Timestamps — the date and time the event was recorded and the date and time the report was synchronised.

Information we do not request: STURNET does not require a user account and does not ask for your name, email address, telephone number, or another personal identifier as part of a bycatch report.

3

Device Features We Use

  • Location — used when you choose to record where a bycatch or sighting event occurred. Coordinates included in a report are uploaded when you synchronise that report.
  • Local storage — new reports are stored in a local database on your device before synchronisation.
  • Internet access — used to send completed reports to the STURNET research server when you select the synchronisation function.
4

Purpose of Collection

Report data is used for scientific research and sturgeon-conservation work by the Protection of Biodiversity in Bulgaria Foundation (PBB Foundation) and affiliated research partners. This may include:

  • Mapping bycatch and sighting events across river systems and coastal areas.
  • Studying sturgeon distribution, condition, and population-health trends.
  • Preparing conservation information for researchers and fisheries authorities.

We do not sell report data, use it for advertising, or share it with commercial advertisers.

5

Where Reports Are Stored

On your device: When you submit a report form, the report is saved to a local database with a pending status. It does not leave your device at this stage.

On the STURNET server: When you open Saved Reports and select Sync Now, pending report data is uploaded to a MySQL database hosted on the STURNET server at sturnet.eu in the European Union and operated for the project by the PBB Foundation. The report is then marked as synced.

Reports that you do not synchronise remain on your device and are not sent to the server.

6

Data Retention & Deletion

Unsynchronised reports: Reports that have not been synchronised exist only on your device. Uninstalling STURNET deletes this local app data.

Synchronised reports: Uninstalling the app does not remove reports that have already been uploaded to the STURNET server. To request the deletion of a synchronised report, email sturnetproject@gmail.com. Because reports do not contain a user account identifier, include the approximate report date, species, and GPS location when available so the record can be located. We aim to process valid deletion requests within 30 days.

7

Children's Privacy

STURNET is a professional conservation and research tool and is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly request personal information from children.

8

Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy as the application and project services evolve. Material changes will be shown on this page with a revised effective date.

9

Contact

For questions about this Privacy Policy or requests concerning a synchronised report, contact:

STURNET Project Privacy Contact sturnetproject@gmail.com
STURNET
